hints.ad: 'ad' is a preposition but does not appear in this phrase

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'ducit' — 'Duc' is a command form
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'ducat' — 'Duc' is the imperative singular
hints.perfect: Perfect would be 'duxit' — 'Duc' is the imperative

hints.dico, dicere, dixi, dictum: These are principal parts of 'dico' (to say)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are principal parts of 'do' (to give)
hints.doceo, docere, docui, doctum: These are principal parts of 'doceo' (to teach)

hints.nominative: Nominative neuter 'altum' has the same form, but after 'in' + motion it is accusative
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'alti' — 'altum' after 'in' expressing motion is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'alti' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'alti' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'alti' ends in -i
